signals.name: Boundary Phase Coherence
signals.description: Mask boundaries should align with phase-only reconstruction edges or phase-gradient discontinuities.
signals.interpretable_rule: Mean phase-edge response on predicted contour should be higher than response inside smooth foreground/background interiors.

signals.name: Edge Alignment
signals.description: Predicted contours should overlap strong image gradients.
signals.interpretable_rule: Contour pixels with high Sobel/Canny response indicate visually plausible boundaries.

signals.name: Interior-Exterior Texture Contrast
signals.description: Foreground texture should differ from the immediate exterior ring.
signals.interpretable_rule: A plausible object mask separates regions with different edge or high-frequency statistics.

signals.name: Frequency Compactness
signals.description: Foreground mask and masked image patch should have plausible frequency distribution.
signals.interpretable_rule: Over-fragmented or noisy masks produce excessive high-frequency mask energy.

signals.name: Spectral Residual Saliency
signals.description: Predicted foreground should overlap regions that are salient under spectral residual analysis.
signals.interpretable_rule: Objects often produce local spectral residual peaks relative to surrounding background.

signals.name: Confidence-Boundary Consistency
signals.description: Pixel confidence should be high in stable interiors and lower near boundaries.
signals.interpretable_rule: High entropy is visually expected near boundaries, not in uniform interiors.

signals.name: Multi-Scale Mask Stability
signals.description: Predicted masks should remain stable under image resizing.
signals.interpretable_rule: Real object regions should survive scale changes better than texture artifacts.

signals.name: TTA Agreement
signals.description: Predictions should agree under reversible transforms.
signals.interpretable_rule: Flip/crop/color-jitter consistency is directly human-verifiable for dense labels.

signals.name: Connected Component Plausibility
signals.description: Foreground components should have plausible area, aspect ratio, and compactness.
signals.interpretable_rule: Tiny islands, excessive fragments, or implausible component shapes often indicate FP regions.

signals.name: Shape Prior
signals.description: Class-specific masks should match known shape statistics.
signals.interpretable_rule: A calibrated class prior over area, eccentricity, compactness, and holes can suppress visually implausible masks.

signals.name: Hole/Boundary Topology Penalty
signals.description: Penalizes implausible holes, broken boundaries, and fragmented topology.
signals.interpretable_rule: Many segmentation classes have simple topology; excessive holes/fragments are usually artifacts.

signals.name: Entropy-Edge Coupling
signals.description: Prediction entropy should correlate with actual image edges.
signals.interpretable_rule: Uncertainty is acceptable at visual boundaries; interior uncertainty signals poor segmentation.
